meaning: n.碎屑,面包屑,少许vt.搓碎,弄碎
examples: She flicked a crumb off the corner of her mouth.
I've nothing to give you—nothing—not a crumb!
Dr. Smith salvaged the last crumb of my self-esteem with this compassionate statement.
The cookies were still warm as we ate them, crumb by crumb, sitting on my bed.
Embedded in the glass is a crumb of coal hauled up from the wreck.
